rulings.Case Title: BERNADETTE MCMENAMIN VS. NORTHERN TRUST, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, A FEDERALLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order; Req. For Judicial Ntc And Appendix Of Non-California Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 5, 2012, Line 1. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted, without prejudice to Plaintiff re-noticing Ms. Barrat's deposition at a later date. Under Liberty Mutual Ins. Co. v. Sup.Ct., (1993) 10 Cal.App.4th 1282, before a deposition of a corporate president or other high level corporate official can be taken it must be established that the deponent has unique or superior personal knowledge of discoverable information. If not, Plaintiff must first obtain the necessary discovery through less intrusive methods including interrogatories or depositions of lower level employees or the corporation itself. Should these avenues be exhausted, and Plaintiff makes a colorable showing of good cause that the high-level official possesses necessary information to the case, the deposition can go forward. In this instance, Ms. Barrat states that she was not involved in determining that Plaintiff should be one of the individuals that was terminated during a reduction in force. Plaintiff¿s primary reason for taking Ms. Barrat¿s deposition is to determine whether her performance reviews of Steven Bell criticized Bell for his handling of Plaintiff¿s termination. At this juncture, it¿s speculative whether Ms. Barrat ever criticized Mr. Bell for his handling of Plaintiff¿s dismissal. Additionally, it appears that Ms. Barrat¿s performance review of Mr. Bell has recently been produced to Plaintiff and Mr. Bell has yet to be deposed. Under Liberty Mutual, Plaintiff must first exhaust other methods of discovery before proceeding with Ms. Barrat¿s deposition. Plaintiff is not precluded from ever deposing Ms. Barrat, only that Plaintiff must exhaust other discovery first, and adduce evidence that contradicts Ms. Barrat¿s declaration. Since Plaintiff has made a reasonable argument in opposition to the motion (though ultimately not successful,) the request for sanctions is denied. The Kent declaration submitted in opposition to the motion will be ordered sealed since it contains confidential information. Defendant¿s request for judicial notice is granted. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WENDY DUJARDIN VS. RODAN AND FIELDS, LLC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 5, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANT RODAN & FIELDS, LLC (INCORRECTLY SUED AND SERVED HEREIN AS RODAN AND FIELDS LLC, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION) MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. Because plaintiff alleges that defendant acted maliciously, she only needs to prove that her fear of developing cancer was reasonable, not that it is more likely than not that she will develop cancer. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a reasonable fear of developing cancer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LARISA PIRIYEVA VS. SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 5, 2012, Line 14, DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the first cause of action and overruled as to the second, fifth, sixth, ninth and tenth causes of action. As to the first cause of action, it cannot be ascertained whether the contract is written, oral or implied by conduct. The other causes of action are adequately pled. None of the causes of action are uncertain. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LARISA PIRIYEVA VS. SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 5, 2012, Line 14, DEFENDANT SINAI MEMORIAL CHAPEL MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. It is improper to move to strike an entire cause of action for failure to state facts sufficient. Moreover, while the eighth cause of action may not be an independent cause of action, it seeks relief tat is available under other causes of action. The prayer for punitive damages is not improper. Sinai Memorial Chapel's status as a religious corporation does not appear on the face of the First Amended Complaint and the prayer for punitive damages is adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
